evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Review a Java 21 change that replaces a value class with `record Digest(byte[] bytes)` and `record SearchResult(List<String> hits)`. Existing behavior requires content equality and forbids caller mutation from changing stored state. Produce `record-semantics-review.md`; do not edit code.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-ranked review that identifies array reference equality and shallow mutability, checks collection ownership/null behavior, recommends the smallest compatible model, and specifies focused equality/aliasing/serialization tests.
assertions: Explains that generated record equality compares arrays by reference.
Explains that record accessors expose mutable array/collection components unless copied.
Does not recommend records mechanically for entities or identity-bearing objects.
Names tests for distinct equal arrays, constructor aliasing, accessor aliasing, and wire compatibility.
Separates confirmed contract violations from preferences.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. A refactor changed `collect(Collectors.toList())` to `stream.toList()` and the result is later sorted and appended to. Write `stream-mutability-plan.md` with the smallest behavior-preserving fix and tests.
evals.expected_output: A plan that treats result mutability as a contract, identifies the runtime failure, chooses a mutable collector/copy or removes mutation based on ownership, and protects ordering, duplicates, null behavior, and call sites.
assertions: States that Stream.toList returns an unmodifiable list.
Does not describe the change as style-only.
Preserves ordering and duplicate policy.
Adds a focused mutation regression test.
Avoids unrelated stream modernization.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Assess a Java 21 Quarkus service that puts every endpoint on virtual threads, performs CPU-heavy hashing, keeps 2 MB ThreadLocal buffers, uses a 20-connection database pool, and blocks inside synchronized code. Produce `virtual-thread-assessment.md`.
evals.expected_output: A JDK-specific assessment separating I/O and CPU work, rejecting virtual-thread pooling/unbounded concurrency, bounding database access, reviewing ThreadLocal allocation and Java 21–23 pinning, and proposing JFR/load verification without claiming universal speedup.
assertions: Distinguishes I/O-bound from CPU-bound work.
States that virtual threads do not remove downstream pool limits.
Reviews ThreadLocal memory amplification.
Makes synchronized pinning advice specific to Java 21–23 and does not blindly rewrite locks.
Requires measurement/JFR/load evidence.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. A Quarkus REST method returns `Uni<Response>`, is annotated `@NonBlocking`, calls Hibernate ORM Panache `Person.listAll()`, and reads a local file before returning. Write `event-loop-fix-plan.md` preserving the HTTP contract.
evals.expected_output: A plan that proves the blocking/event-loop mismatch, chooses a consistent blocking worker or supported virtual-thread path (or a truly reactive stack), preserves transactions and HTTP mapping, and adds a Quarkus integration test plus thread/blocking evidence.
assertions: Identifies Hibernate ORM and file I/O as blocking operations.
Does not fix the issue merely by wrapping values in Uni.
Names the project Quarkus version/dispatch rules as a prerequisite.
Preserves status/body/error/transaction behavior.
Specifies a framework-level verification path.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Diagnose a Quarkus ArC startup failure: two producers of the same type use `@Named("fast")` and `@Named("safe")`, a default injection point is ambiguous, and another bean from a dependency is not discovered. Programmatic lookup also disappears in native mode. Produce `arc-diagnosis.md`.
evals.expected_output: A diagnosis covering @Named/@Default ambiguity, type-safe qualifiers or Identifier, Jandex/index-dependency/producer discovery, programmatic lookup and unused-bean removal, and precise augmentation/native tests without globally disabling removal.
assertions: Explains the @Named default-qualifier ambiguity in Quarkus CDI.
Recommends a type-safe qualifier or supported Identifier for internal selection.
Checks dependency indexing/bean-defining annotations.
Checks unused-bean removal visibility for programmatic lookup.
Avoids global bean-removal disable as the first fix.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Review Hibernate Reactive Panache code using Jakarta `@Transactional`, calling `.await().indefinitely()`, and publishing a message in `invoke()` before commit. Write `reactive-transaction-review.md`.
evals.expected_output: A review that identifies the reactive transaction model, removes blocking waits, composes terminal completion, analyzes commit-before-publication and idempotency/outbox choices, and defines rollback/cancellation/redelivery tests.
assertions: Uses the project-supported reactive transaction API such as @WithTransaction rather than assuming Jakarta @Transactional.
Rejects await/blocking on the reactive path.
Explains that side effects before commit can be inconsistent.
Considers outbox/idempotency rather than claiming Java transaction atomicity with the broker.
Tests asynchronous success, failure, rollback, and cancellation.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. A Quarkus app works on the JVM but fails native: it constructs classes from configuration with `Class.forName`, loads `templates/invoice.html`, and has static fields initialized from `Instant.now()` and a random secret. Produce `native-readiness-plan.md`.
evals.expected_output: A precise native plan that identifies dynamic reachability, resource inclusion, and build-time static initialization; favors explicit factories/extension metadata and precise registration; removes secret/time capture; and requires a native integration test.
assertions: Does not register an entire package for reflection without evidence.
Handles the class-loading design rather than only adding metadata.
Adds the exact resource and verifies packaged/native loading.
Moves time/random/secret initialization to runtime-owned dependencies.
Names native build/run verification separately from JVM tests.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Assess a Quarkus endpoint whose feature toggle is build-time fixed but operations expect to change it with a runtime env var; the endpoint logs bearer tokens, checks only a role but not account ownership, and tags metrics by account ID. Produce `security-config-observability-review.md`.
evals.expected_output: An evidence-ranked review covering configuration phase/package testing, secret redaction/rotation, separate authn/authz ownership checks, bounded metric cardinality, and integration tests for denial and runtime behavior.
assertions: Distinguishes build-time and runtime configuration.
Requires packaged-mode proof for the override.
Treats bearer-token logging as a security issue and mentions rotation if exposed.
Tests cross-account authorization, not just roles.
Removes unbounded account-ID metric labels.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Review a Quarkus ORM refactor that returns entities from REST, generated equals/hashCode over all fields, changes a relation to EAGER to fix lazy loading, calls a payment API inside `@Transactional`, and edits an already-deployed Flyway migration. Produce `persistence-risk-assessment.md`.
evals.expected_output: A staged, severity-ranked assessment protecting entity identity, HTTP DTO boundaries, query count, transaction/external side effects, constraints/idempotency, and immutable migration history with concrete database and HTTP tests.
assertions: Rejects entity-as-REST coupling with a focused response model.
Analyzes mutable entity equality and hash stability.
Does not accept global EAGER as the default lazy-load fix.
Analyzes payment call atomicity/idempotency outside database transaction semantics.
Requires a new migration rather than rewriting deployed history.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. A repository targets Java 17 and an older Quarkus BOM. A proposed cleanup uses Java 21 record patterns, a config key from current Quarkus docs, and adds an independently versioned extension to make imports compile. Write `version-safety-review.md`.
evals.expected_output: A review that treats repository toolchain/BOM/effective dependencies as source of truth, rejects accidental upgrades and invented dependencies, offers Java 17/project-version alternatives, and defines compile/consumer gates.
assertions: Does not force Java 21 because the skill has a Java 21 baseline.
Checks effective Maven/Gradle and Quarkus platform versions.
Rejects adding a random dependency to satisfy a guessed API.
Separates an intentional upgrade into its own change.
Uses repository wrapper and affected-module/dependent compilation.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java. Review an agent patch for a three-line bug: it adds six interfaces, two builders, a mapper dependency, reformats 80 files, upgrades plugins, deletes the failing test, and adds interaction-only mock tests. Produce `patch-scope-review.md`.
evals.expected_output: An adversarial scope review that restores the regression test, isolates the root-cause fix, removes abstraction/dependency/format churn, demands outcome-based proof, and uses diff/build gates without declaring scanner matches defects.
assertions: Flags test deletion as high risk.
Rejects abstraction inflation without a variation/boundary.
Separates build upgrades and formatting from the bugfix.
Requires defect, fix, and unchanged-behavior proof.
Prefers outcome/state assertions over mock-interaction theater.

evals.prompt: Use $effective-java to produce `java-quarkus-assessment.md` for a mixed Java 21 Quarkus service. You can inspect files but cannot run Docker or native builds. Classify every finding by evidence/confidence, propose staged changes, and report exact unrun gates honestly.
evals.expected_output: A concise assessment using a project profile, evidence-backed top risks, a minimal target shape, dependency-aware batches, exact wrapper commands, explicit Docker/native limitations, and no claims that unavailable gates passed.
assertions: Profiles Java, build tool, Quarkus BOM/extensions, execution and persistence models.
Uses severity and confidence and separates candidates/preferences from defects.
Gives a verification command or proof for each batch.
States Docker/native gates were not run and limits claims accordingly.
Avoids broad framework or architecture rewrites without evidence.
